top_signals.title: Oil Prices Are Crashing. Inventories Tell A Different Story.
top_signals.summary: Oil traders have gone from pricing in the worst supply disruption in modern history to pricing in a recovery that hasn't happened yet. Inventory data suggests they’re getting ahead of themselves. Brent crude fell below $77 per barrel on Thursday, the lowest level since the early days of the Middle East war, after the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz and launching 60 days of negotiations toward a broader agreement. The market's verdict was immediate: sell first, ask questions…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: OPEC Maintains Robust Oil Growth Forecast Over Next Four Years
top_signals.summary: OPEC maintained its forecast for robust global oil demand growth in the next four years on Thursday and nudged up its longer-term view, citing a worldwide shift towards more supportive policies for oil use and saying there was no sign demand…

top_signals.title: Warsh promises sweeping Fed review after central bank hints at rate hike this year
top_signals.summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh on Wednesday outlined a slew of task forces being set up to look at current central bank practices and said policymakers would drop the issuance of forward guidance in the future. Calling it a “new chapter for the central bank” for financial markets, Warsh emphasized that the monetary policy ...

top_signals.title: Mining’s nuclear reality check: SMRs are still not on miners’ shopping lists
top_signals.summary: Small and micro modular reactors are at the very bottom on miners’ low-carbon energy plans. That is striking given the hype around SMRs, the AI-driven nuclear revival and the increasingly bullish outlook for uranium.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: The World Cup: Players Vie for Goals and Countries for Influence
top_signals.summary:
The 2026 FIFA Men’s World Cup is now well underway, bringing 48 countries’ teams to compete in a soccer tournament co-hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada. With teams from every continent except Antarctica, the 2026 tournament is a truly global event. The World Cup is often hailed as an opportunity for the world to come together and celebrate a shared passion for “the beautiful game” — creating an opportunity every four years for countries to gain attention, shape their reputation, and build influence.Although the joint U.S.-Mexican-Canadian bid intended to demonstrate North American connections, relations have deteriorated since the

top_signals.title: Extra-EU exports of final services peaked in 2025
top_signals.summary: In 2025, extra-EU exports of final services reached a record level of +61 percentage points (pp) above 2017 levels, up from +55 pp in 2024. Final services reflect a broad mix of activities, with travel-related services among the leading contributors, alongside ICT, media, computer, business and financial services.
